decks.notes: The main idea behind this Dimir Control deck is to efficiently control the opponent's actions through countering spells, removing creatures, and card advantage. Sneaky Snacker and Tolarian Terror provide early aggression, while Gurmag Angler acts as a late game finisher. Utilizing powerful control spells like Counterspell, Snuff Out, and Cast Out, along with card draw cards like Brainstorm and Deep Analysis, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and outvalue them in the long run. The mana base includes Contaminated Aquifer and Ice Tunnel to support the two-color mana requirements.

The focus of this deck is to aggressively burn the opponent's life total by utilizing cheap spells like Lightning Bolt, Lava Dart, and Fireblast, while also using creatures like Voldaren Epicure and Sneaky Snacker to apply pressure. Faithless Looting helps in filtering through the deck for key cards, while Grab the Prize and Highway Robbery provide additional burn and card advantage. The goal is to quickly deplete the opponent's life total before they have a chance to stabilize and win the game.

The deck revolves around flooding the board with low-cost elves to generate mana quickly and overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of creatures. It utilizes cards like Priest of Titania to generate even more mana, while also incorporating cards like Timberwatch Elf to pump up the team and close out games quickly. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help refill the hand with more elves to keep the pressure on. Masked Vandal is included for removal and disruption, while Nyxborn Hydra and Avenging Hunter provide big threats to finish off the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to effectively swarm the battlefield with small creatures and overwhelm the opponent with a steady stream of low-cost, efficient threats. By utilizing cards that generate multiple creature tokens, as well as utilizing bounce effects and combat tricks, the deck aims to maintain board presence and apply pressure early and often. Additionally, the inclusion of a few utility cards such as removal and graveyard hate helps to disrupt the opponent's game plan and pave the way for a swift victory.

This Dimir Control deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's plans through a combination of counterspells, removal spells, and card draw. It aims to control the board with efficient creatures like Faerie Seer and Gurmag Angler, while maintaining card advantage with spells like Brainstorm and Deep Analysis. The deck also includes bounce lands for mana fixing and utility lands like Bojuka Bog for graveyard hate. Overall, the strategy revolves around maintaining control of the game and out-valuing the opponent in the long run.

The main idea of this deck is to use creatures and spells to control the board and manipulate the graveyard, while generating value through sacrificing and recurring creatures. It focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy with removal spells and graveyard hate, while also maintaining a board presence with resilient creatures like Troll of Khazad-dûm and Avenging Hunter. The deck also includes card draw and lifegain elements to help ensure longevity in the game. The Golgari / Jund color combination allows for a versatile control strategy that can adapt to different matchups.

This deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with efficient and resilient creatures like Glistener Elf and Blight Mamba. By using pump spells like Might of Old Krosa and Mutagenic Growth, the deck aims to push through damage and poison counters rapidly. Snakeskin Veil protects key creatures from removal, while Rancor helps maintain pressure. Embiggen boosts the power of all creatures, allowing for explosive turns. Escape Tunnel provides a way to protect creatures from removal, ensuring the aggro strategy can continue uninterrupted.

The main focus of this Pauper 1 vs 1 deck is to aggressively attack and put pressure on the opponent using a variety of low-cost, efficient creatures and burn spells. By quickly bringing out creatures like Clockwork Percussionist and Goblin Tomb Raider, as well as utilizing direct damage spells like Chain Lightning and Lightning Bolt, the deck aims to deal quick and consistent damage to the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Reckless Impulse and Wrenn's Resolve allows for card advantage and protection, while Experimental Synthesizer provides additional utility. The deck is designed to overwhelm the opponent with fast, aggressive plays and efficient removal spells.

This deck focuses on utilizing elves to quickly overwhelm the opponent with aggressive creatures. The main strategy involves generating mana through cards like Arbor Elf and Priest of Titania to cast powerful creatures like Nyxborn Hydra and Timberwatch Elf. Lead the Stampede and Winding Way help refill the hand with elves to maintain a steady stream of threats. The goal is to build a wide board presence and grow the creatures' power with abilities like Timberwatch Elf's pump effect to quickly close out the game.

The deck focuses on low-cost creatures and efficient mana production to quickly overwhelm opponents with a strong board presence. It also includes cards that provide card advantage and combat tricks to maintain pressure on the opponent. The inclusion of cards like Leonardo, Big Brother and Spider-Man, Web-Slinger adds additional utility and versatility to the deck. The deck aims to maintain a consistent aggressive strategy while also having options for resilience and card advantage in longer games.

This Pauper 1 vs 1 Aggro - Affinity deck focuses on utilizing artifacts cards and sacrificing them for various effects such as card draw, damage, and disruption. The deck includes cards like Pactdoll Terror and Krark-Clan Shaman to generate value from sacrificing artifacts, along with utility cards like Cleansing Wildfire and Nihil Spellbomb for disrupting the opponent's strategy. The synergies between artifact creatures and spellbombs provide a versatile and aggressive playstyle designed to overwhelm opponents quickly. The mana base is designed to support the deck's fast-paced strategy while incorporating a mix of colors for flexibility.

The main theme of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with a fast and aggressive playstyle using efficient creatures and ramp spells. The deck utilizes cards like Arbor Elf, Utopia Sprawl, and Wild Growth to accelerate mana production and quickly drop threats like Annoyed Altisaur and Avenging Hunter. Eldrazi Repurposer and Mwonvuli Acid-Moss disrupt the opponent's resources, while Malevolent Rumble and Writhing Chrysalis provide removal and creature generation. The overall strategy of this Gruul Aggro deck is to apply pressure early and maintain a strong board presence to secure victory.

This deck focuses on quickly putting pressure on the opponent with aggressive creatures and removal spells. It aims to control the board with efficient creature removal and disruption while also generating card advantage with artifacts. The deck utilizes sacrifice effects to gain additional value and provide reach in the late game. The mana base allows for consistent casting of spells while providing options for card draw and fixing. Overall, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of aggression, disruption, and card advantage.

This deck focuses on using aggressive Jund-colored creatures and removal spells to control the board and chip away at the opponent's life total. The deck includes cards like Cast Down and Terminate to deal with threats, while also utilizing Drossforge Bridge and Writhing Chrysalis to generate value. Fanatical Offering and Ichor Wellspring provide card advantage, while Gixian Infiltrator and Refurbished Familiar offer efficient threats. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of removal, card advantage, and efficient creatures to come out on top in a fast-paced game.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming your opponent with aggressive creatures and removal spells. By utilizing a combination of efficient removal spells like Cast Down and Cleansing Wildfire, alongside powerful creatures like Gixian Infiltrator and Krark-Clan Shaman, you aim to control the battlefield while steadily building card advantage through cards like Ichor Wellspring and Refurbished Familiar. By disrupting your opponent's game plan and applying pressure early on, you aim to close out the game before your opponent has a chance to stabilize.

This Jund Aggro deck focuses on disrupting opponent's strategies with removal spells like Cast Down and board control cards like Cleansing Wildfire. It also utilizes artifacts like Nihil Spellbomb and Ichor Wellspring to generate card advantage. The deck aims to apply pressure with efficient creatures like Krark-Clan Shaman and Nyxborn Hydra, while also having some reach with direct damage cards like Makeshift Munitions and Eviscerator's Insight. Additionally, the deck can recycle creatures with Refurbished Familiar and gain life with cards like Pulse of Murasa to outlast opponents in the long game.
